hints.across-1: The open country near or belonging to a town or city.
hints.down-2: Fine, dry particles of matter found in the air and covering the surface of objects, typically consisting of soil lifted up by the wind, pollen, hair, etc.
hints.across-3: Open area used for gathering in a city, often having small trees and sitting benches.
hints.down-4: Drift of a ship or aeroplane in a leeward direction.
hints.down-5: A fixed one-dimensional figure, such as a line or arc, with an origin and orientation and such that its points are in one-to-one correspondence with a set of numbers; an axis forms part of the basis of a space or is used to position and locate data in a graph
hints.across-7: Surroundings of, and influences on, a particular item of interest.
hints.across-8: Nimbus, a luminous disc, often of gold, around or over the heads of saints, etc., in religious paintings.
hints.down-9: The authorized form of ceremonial worship of a church.
hints.across-10: The highest point in the sky reached by a celestial body.
hints.across-11: Any instrument used in astronomy for observing distant objects.
